tweets.text:
Fascinating that the radio is the third fastest technology in history to reach 50% penetration

It spread faster than the internet (only AI and the smartphone beat it)

But the dishwasher took 47 years

I loved Alex's analogy for the two models of adoption, the radio and the dishwasher:

"We commissioned Horace Dediu, who used to work with Clayton Christensen, to go look at S-curves over the last 100 years.

The radio S-curve is one of the fastest ever. But the dishwasher S-curve is the opposite, because it needs to be plugged into the back end. 

B2B stuff can take a long time, because it needs to be plugged into the existing systems. Consumers generally tend to go a lot faster.

I covered B2B internet at Fidelity. 

The underlying infrastructure wasn't in place for B2B to happen. It ultimately happened 20 years later, with SaaS.

That is a risk with AI, in that these big companies are very security-conscious and can be slow to move. 

There are a lot of cultural issues with AI, where you really need a few evangelists to push it through. 

The top management needs to push it through, but then IT is saying this is risky. And that happened with cloud too.

Everybody was afraid that it's not secure to have your data in the cloud. And then we saw the CIA do it, and we saw Capital One. 

We talked to the Capital One CIO, who said it's more secure in the cloud. And then it really started to take off.

But what's amazing about AI is that you just open up the browser and it's there. And so that's why we're getting this straight up, we call it the backwards L-curve."
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
My conversation with Alex Sacerdote, founder of Whale Rock Capital Management.

Alex runs more than $17B and has been one of the best performing tech investors for years, though he keeps a low public profile.

As you'll hear, he is singular in how he thinks about investing https://t.co/0jB6r7oWPm

tweets.text:
Meanwhile, @LidoFinance lost ~26% TVL in the last 30d.

Imo, it’s more about the whole Ethereum Foundation identity crisis.

Look, ETH went from ~$2,306 on May 9 to around ~$1,649 today, and that’s roughly a 28% drop, while Lido’s TVL fell around 26% over the same period.

When ETH drops, Lido’s TVL drops too, as people want to exit their ETH positions.

Snowball effect.

tweets.text:
One of the things that makes Skyharbour Resources  $SYH / $SYH.V unique in the #uranium sector is its ability to create leverage through its prospect generator model.

Think of it like owning an apartment building.

Most investors focus on the value of the building itself. But what if several tenants were paying to renovate and improve different units, while you retained ownership and benefited from the increase in value?

That's essentially what Skyharbour has built across its uranium portfolio.

🔹 Multiple partner-funded drill programs
🔹 Reduced capital requirements
🔹 Exposure to discovery upside across numerous projects
🔹 Continued advancement of core assets like Russell Lake and Moore

As exploration activity accelerates across the Athabasca Basin, Skyharbour shareholders gain exposure to multiple opportunities for discovery and value creation without the company having to fund every meter of drilling itself.
The beauty of the model is that every successful drill campaign has the potential to create value, while Skyharbour remains focused on advancing its flagship projects.
With several partner-funded programs and its own drilling underway, Skyharbour offers something increasingly valuable in the uranium sector: multiple shots at discovery, multiple catalysts, and multiple paths to success.

Disclosure: Paid partnership with Skyharbour Resources. Not financial advice. Please do your own due diligence.

tweets.text:
Early stage crypto investing is dead.

Pre-Seed and Seed rounds used to fund this entire industry. Today the money is all late-stage and M&A.

Everyone midcurving this chart sees the end of crypto VC, but what it actually shows:

1) Early stage crypto is the least competitive it's been since 2019 = much less bidding wars, FOMO rounds or inflated terms

2) The ratio of quality founders to capital chasing them has never been this attractive, hence they're raising at floor valuations

3) Thanks to AI, a 5 person team now ships what used to take 50. Seed dollars have never gone further

4) Capital stopped being the differentiator. What founders actually need is hands-on partners, and barely anyone is left providing that (= Moonrock)

While the rest of the industry is mourning early stage, I see a shopping paradise ahead of us.

We're doubling down.

tweets.full_text:
Early stage crypto investing is dead.

Pre-Seed and Seed rounds used to fund this entire industry. Today the money is all late-stage and M&A.

Everyone midcurving this chart sees the end of crypto VC, but what it actually shows:

1) Early stage crypto is the least competitive it's been since 2019 = much less bidding wars, FOMO rounds or inflated terms

2) The ratio of quality founders to capital chasing them has never been this attractive, hence they're raising at floor valuations

3) Thanks to AI, a 5 person team now ships what used to take 50. Seed dollars have never gone further

4) Capital stopped being the differentiator. What founders actually need is hands-on partners, and barely anyone is left providing that (= Moonrock)

While the rest of the industry is mourning early stage, I see a shopping paradise ahead of us.

We're doubling down.


tweets.text:
Everyone watches what dumps hardest in a crash.

I watch what bounces first.

The fastest recoveries tell you what's being quietly accumulated while everyone else panics.

What's interesting is 7 of the top 14 bounces came from one sector.

Here's the full list and what it's telling you:

• @Velvet_Capital ($165M, +71%) - AI-powered trading and portfolio management.

• @readycards ($14M, +44%) - Crypto wallet and card for daily spending.

• @xmaquina ($18M, +37%) - DAO for investing in real robotics and humanoid AI companies using crypto.

• @StargateFinance ($41M, +29%) - The LayerZero bridge, and the largest app built on that infrastructure.

• @Zcash ($7.2B, +27%) - Got hit hard by the Orchard Vulnerability but recovered fast.

• @KeetaNetwork ($99M, +21%) - Purpose-built for fiat payments and bank-to-crypto connectivity. The recent pump came off their ASK Group collab announcement.

• @worldnetwork ($1.7B, +21%) - Human proof in the AI era via eye scan verification.

• @NEARProtocol ($2.7B, +15%) - Pivoted hard toward open infrastructure for the agent economy.

• @Pumpfun ($529M, +14%) - Pumpfun GO just launched and it's pulling real onchain activity.

• @HyperliquidX ($12.6B, +13%) - Spending $880M/year buying back HYPE. One of the best performers all year, not just this bounce.

• @OndoFinance ($1.7B, +12%) - Accounts for over 25% of all tokenized treasury market cap on its own.

• @opentensor ($2B, +11%) - The best breakthrough tech in DeAI is still coming out of Bittensor and its ecosystem.

• @USDai_Official ($74M, +11%) - Stablecoin backed by real AI hardware like GPUs.

• @Unibase_AI ($355M, +10%) - Decentralized storage giving AI agents long-term memory.

IMO it's not time to bid yet. Conditions are way too choppy. I watch what bounces back to build my watchlist.

If something keeps showing up on this list and it's not on my radar, that's my cue to start digging.

tweets.text:
Headed to DC for a full day of meetings.   I still am an optimist and think we will get this Clarity act over the hill.   Senators on both sides have spent an inordinate amount of time on this bill.  It’s a very complicated piece of legislation and we are down to two or three issues where the solution seems fair and good for our country. 

The division in our country makes agreement on an issue difficult.   Feels like this could be a bright spot in showing the American people that congress can still work together.   

This is it though.  The next four weeks is all we have.  Then summer recess, and election season which is gonna get more divisive.  

I appreciate all the efforts the industry has put in on this.  The burden of change does not get shared equally.  The people in the DC trenches have a patience and persistence that is admirable.

Teams don't like to launch in risk-off environments.

tweets.text:
The praise for what is happening in Belfast is astonishing. 

Everyone should be outraged at a beheading on the street. 

But burning innocent people’s homes and terrorising the streets is not the answer. 

Most people are ignoring of Belfast’s past. 

There are many gangs of hoodlums who hear stories of their parents and want to relive the troubles. They pop up from time to time and cause chaos. This is just another instance, but actually covered in the news. 

All the influencers here encouraging that level of hate are disgusting. Especially the folks in crypto. Despicable.
